optionA.text: Peristomium forms a large ventral collar

optionB.text: Peristomium not ventrally enlarged

optionA.text: Some notopodia with pectinate branchiae

optionB.text: Branchiae absent

optionA.text: Branchiae arise from the dorsal cirrus; all setae composite spinigers

optionB.text: Branchiae arise from the notopodial lobes; some composite falcigers present

optionA.text: Anterior ventrum with transverse fleshy ridges

optionB.text: Anterior ventrum smooth

optionA.text: Antennae absent

optionB.text: At least one antenna present

optionA.text: Two pairs of tentacular cirri present; anterior apodous segment absent

optionB.text: Three pairs of tentacular cirri present; anterior apodous segment present

optionA.text: A single median antenna present

optionB.text: Two antennae present

optionA.text: Paragnaths present on the maxillary ring; parapodia biramous (except the first two)

optionB.text: Paragnaths absent; parapodia uniramous

optionA.text: Notocirri of parapodia 5-7 broadly elytraeform

optionB.text: Notocirri of parapodia 5-7 cylindrical and cirriform

optionA.text: Notopodia strongly reduced or absent

optionB.text: Median and posterior notopodia well developed, with lobes and setae

optionA.text: Notosetae present

optionB.text: Notosetae absent

optionA.text: Tentacular cirri articulated

optionB.text: Tentacular cirri smooth

optionA.text: Notacicula present; antennae and cirri well developed

optionB.text: Notacicula absent; antennae and cirri reduced

optionA.text: Eversible pharynx with either papillae or paragnaths or both, in addition to the jaws

optionB.text: Eversible pharynx with jaws, but otherwise smooth

optionA.text: Eversible pharynx with soft papillae only

optionB.text: Eversible pharynx with at least some paragnaths

optionA.text: Two pairs of tentacular cirri; apodous segment absent

optionB.text: Four pairs of tentacular cirri; apodous segment present

optionA.text: Dorsal cirri attached basally on the notopodial superior lobes

optionB.text: Dorsal cirri attached distally on the notopodial superior lobes

optionA.text: Notopodial homogomph falcigers present in posterior setigers

optionB.text: Notopodial homogomph falcigers absent

optionA.text: Superior notopodial lobes long and straplike; inferior neuropodial lobe absent

optionB.text: Superior notopodial lobes large and foliose; inferior neuropodial lobes present

optionA.text: Pharyngeal papillae at least in part in tufts

optionB.text: Pharyngeal papillae solitary

optionA.text: All setae homogomph spinigers

optionB.text: Setae include also neuropodial homogomph falcigers in posterior setigers

optionA.text: Ventral cirri double at least in some setigers

optionB.text: All ventral cirri simple

optionA.text: Accessory dorsal cirri on some anterior setigers; posterior dorsal cirri long and whiplike

optionB.text: Accessory dorsal cirri absent; posterior dorsal cirri not whiplike

optionA.text: Pharyngeal papillae on both rings; inferior neuropodial lobes absent; dorsal cirri distally attached

optionB.text: Pharyngeal papillae on oral ring only; inferior neuropodial lobes present; dorsal cirri basally attached

optionA.text: Eversible pharynx with both papillae and paragnaths

optionB.text: Papillae absent, paragnaths present

optionA.text: Paragnaths present on one pharyngeal ring only

optionB.text: Paragnaths present on both pharyngeal rings

optionA.text: Paragnaths present on the maxillary ring only

optionB.text: Paragnaths present on the oral ring only

optionA.text: Paragnaths in eight groups, all rod-shaped

optionB.text: Paragnaths in patches and bands, all conical

optionA.text: Notopodial homogomph falcigers present in posterior setigers

optionB.text: Notopodial homogomph falcigers absent

optionA.text: All paragnaths conical

optionB.text: Cones and in addition either transverse or pectinate paragnaths or both present on the pharynx

optionA.text: All setae homogomph spinigers

optionB.text: At least some falcigers present

optionA.text: Middle and posterior neuropodia with single homogomph falcigers

optionB.text: Homogomph falcigers, if present, in notopodial positions

optionA.text: With blunt simple falcigers in notopodia

optionB.text: Simple falcigers absent, composite falcigers present or absent

optionA.text: Notopodial homogomph falcigers present in posterior setigers

optionB.text: Notopodial homogomph falcigers absent

optionA.text: Paragnaths include pectinate bars and usually small patches of cones; transverse smooth bars absent

optionB.text: Paragnaths include transverse smooth bars, patches of cones and sometimes pectinate bars

optionA.text: Superior notopodial lobes greatly expanded in posterior setigers; pectinate bars usually present

optionB.text: Superior notopodial lobes not expanded in any setigers; pectinate bars absent
